data.content_ref: inline:The pandemic mechanism requires the pathogen to selectively kill the administrative and craft-specialist classes while sparing the rural and peripheral populations who then opportunistically occupied the centers. No known historical pandemic exhibits this class-selective lethality; diseases spread through trade networks typically affect all exposed populations, and dense urban quarters would have included laborers, servants, and other non-elites who would also have been infected. The account offers no epidemiological basis for why the pathogen would discriminate between administrators and the general urban population, making the selective depopulation of only the ruling apparatus implausible.
